Good morning, Emmanuele. Thank you for the numbers. Let's talk about last quarter.
Good morning, Marc. Last quarter was busy, but we had good news.
Good. Start with the target. What happened?
In dermatology, we exceeded the target. We achieved one hundred and eight percent.
Excellent. And the challenge?
We missed the target in asthma. Market access was slow in the public market.
I see. And where do you see an opportunity?
The growth came from the private market. My team met sixty new doctors, and prescription volume went up.

Hello Emmanuele. Marc Dubois again, from Paris. I looked at your numbers this morning, and I have three questions for August. Number one. What happened with the asthma target last quarter? Number two. What did your team do in market access? And number three. Where did the growth come from? Please do not prepare fifty slides. Three answers. Three sentences each. Last quarter we achieved. Last quarter we missed. Last quarter we learned. That is all I need. Thank you.

Good afternoon, everyone. My name is Claire Bennett, from the global immunology team, and welcome to the quarterly business review. Before the country presentations, here are the global numbers for the last quarter. We exceeded the target in dermatology by six percent. We achieved ninety-four percent of the target in respiratory. And we missed the target in one market, because market access was slower than we expected. The growth came from the private market, and prescription volume went up in four countries. Once in a while, we have a difficult quarter. This was not one of them. Brazil, you are first. The floor is yours.

In April, we started the quarter with a clear target. In May, my team met sixty new doctors in the private market. In June, prescription volume grew and we exceeded the target in dermatology. At the end of the quarter, we missed the target in asthma, because market access was slow. Last week, I presented the results to the Global team.
